Blending.Transparency: This decides whether the thing you are drawing can be seen through, like glass or plastic, or not, like cardboard.
Blending.Blend Operation: This decides how the thing you are drawing mixes with what is already behind it on the screen.
Blending.Source Color: This is the color coming from the material you are using right now.
Blending.Destination Color: This is the color that is already on the screen behind the object.
Blending.Separate Alpha Function: This lets the see-through part follow different rules than the color part.
Blending.Alpha Blend: This controls how see-through the object becomes when mixed with what is behind it.
Blending.Orient: This checks which side of a surface is facing the camera.
Blending.Operation: This is the math rule that decides how colors are mixed together.
Blending.Source Alpha: This is how see-through the object itself is.
Blending.Destination Alpha: This is how see-through the background already is.
Blending.Blend Constant Color: This is a fixed color used for mixing, instead of using the object or background color.
Blending.Blend Constant Alpha: This is a fixed see-through amount used for mixing.
Blending.Legacy Alpha Behavior: This uses older rules that existed in past versions.
Blending.Point Color Premultiply: This mixes color and see-through amount together before drawing.
Blending.Depth Test: This checks which object is closer to the camera.
Blending.Depth Test Function: This decides how closeness is compared.
Blending.Write Depth Values: This tells the computer to remember how close the object is.
Blending.Discard Pixels Based on Alpha: This throws away very faint parts so they are not drawn.
Blending.Keep Pixels With Alpha: This keeps only parts that are strong enough to see.
Blending.Alpha Threshold: This is the cutoff point for what is kept or thrown away.
Blending.Wireframe: This draws only the lines of the shape, like a cage.
Blending.Line Width: This controls how thick those lines are.
Blending.Cull Face: This hides surfaces that are facing the wrong direction.
Blending.Polygon Depth Offset: This slightly moves surfaces forward or backward to stop flickering.

operators.what_it_is: A material that paints an object one solid color without reacting to lights.
operators.mental_model: Dipping an object into a bucket of paint.
operators.example: Make a train car one flat color with no shadows.

menu.Apply Projection Maps: Uses projected images if any exist.
menu.Color: The paint color used on the object.
menu.Alpha: How see-through the object is. Zero means invisible. One means solid.
menu.Apply Point Color: Uses color stored on the object itself.
menu.Color Map: An image that decides color across the surface.
menu.Deform: Allows the surface to bend or move.
menu.Attribute: A named piece of data stored on the object.
menu.Bone Bind Poses: The original pose bones start from.
menu.PCAP Data Attrib: Which data is used for movement.
menu.Skeleton Root Path: The main bone everything connects to.
menu.MAT: Lets this material reference another material.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It colors something one simple color, like crayons.

Deform.Deform: Turns on bending or moving the object using animation data.
Deform.Get Bone Data: Reads movement from a skeleton made of bones.
Deform.POP/SOP With Capture Data: Uses stored movement information from particles or geometry.
Deform.Bone Paths: Tells the material where the bones live.
Deform.PCAP Path: Location of point capture animation data.
Deform.Attribute: Name of the data used to move the object.
Deform.Bone Bind Poses: The original resting pose of the bones.
Deform.Skeleton Root Path: The main bone everything connects to.

operators.what_it_is: A material that draws how far surfaces are from the camera instead of color.
operators.mental_model: A ruler that measures distance instead of paint.
operators.example: Show which parts of a train are closer or farther away.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It doesn’t draw color. It draws distance.

GLSL Core.Load: Loads the shader code from a file or text.
GLSL Core.Version: Tells the computer which language rules to use.
GLSL Core.Preprocessor Directives: Small instructions that change how the code is prepared before running.
GLSL Core.Vertex Shader: Code that decides where points of the shape are placed.
GLSL Core.Pixel Shader: Code that decides the color of each tiny dot on the screen.
GLSL Core.Geometry Shader: Optional code that can create or change shapes.
GLSL Core.Compile Behavior: Controls when the code is checked and prepared.
GLSL Core.Error Behavior: Decides what happens if the code has mistakes.
GLSL Core.Load Uniform Names: Reads outside values so the code can use them.
GLSL Core.Lighting Space: Decides how light direction is understood.
GLSL Core.Attributes: Data stored on the shape itself.
GLSL Core.Attribute Name: Name of the data on the shape.
GLSL Core.Type: What kind of data it is.
GLSL Core.Components: How many parts a value has.
GLSL Core.Columns: How many vertical sections a grid has.
GLSL Core.Samplers: Ways to read images or textures.
GLSL Core.Sampler Name: Name used to access a texture.
GLSL Core.TOP: The image used by the shader.
GLSL Core.Colors: Named color values sent into the code.
GLSL Core.Premultiply RGB by Alpha: Mixes color with see-through amount first.
GLSL Core.RGB Alpha: Red, green, blue, and see-through value.
GLSL Core.Array Type: What kind of values are in the list.
GLSL Core.Constants: Fixed numbers that do not change.
GLSL Core.Attribute Class: What kind of object the data belongs to.
GLSL Core.Name: Label used to find the data.

Comment.Comment: A note for humans that does not affect behavior.

operators.what_it_is: A material where you write instructions that control how shapes are drawn.
operators.mental_model: Giving the computer a recipe for drawing.
operators.example: Make a train surface ripple, glow, or distort using math.

menu.MAT: Allows this material to reference another material.

operators.explain_like_im_five: You tell the computer step by step how to draw something.

GLSL Core.Load: This brings in the instructions you wrote so the computer can use them.
GLSL Core.Version: This tells the computer which rulebook to follow.
GLSL Core.Preprocessor Directives: Little notes that change how the instructions are read before anything starts.
GLSL Core.Vertex Shader: Instructions that decide where the shape goes and how it bends.
GLSL Core.Pixel Shader: Instructions that decide the color of every tiny dot on the screen.
GLSL Core.Geometry Shader: Extra instructions that can add or remove pieces of a shape.
GLSL Core.Compile Behavior: Decides when the computer checks the instructions for mistakes.
GLSL Core.Error Behavior: Decides what the computer does if the instructions are wrong.
GLSL Core.Load Uniform Names: Brings in outside knobs you can turn.
GLSL Core.Inherit Uniform Samplers From: Borrows images and settings from another material.
GLSL Core.Lighting Space: Decides how light direction is understood.
GLSL Core.TD Simplex Noise: A built-in way to make natural-looking messiness, like clouds or waves.
GLSL Core.Attributes: Information attached to the object, like notes taped onto it.
GLSL Core.Type: What kind of note it is.
GLSL Core.Matrix Attribute Name: The name of a big grid of numbers.
GLSL Core.Columns: How many vertical slices a grid has.
GLSL Core.Sampler Name: The name used to talk about an image.
GLSL Core.Premultiply RGB by Alpha: Mixes color and see-through amount together first.
GLSL Core.RGB Alpha: Red, green, blue, and how see-through it is.
GLSL Core.Array Type: What kind of things are in the list.
GLSL Core.Relative Name: A name based on something else.
GLSL Core.Constant Name: The name of a never-changing number.
GLSL Core.Attribute Class: What kind of thing owns the data.
GLSL Core.Name: The label used to find it.

Comment.Comment: A note for humans that the computer ignores.

operators.what_it_is: A material where you write your own drawing rules.
operators.mental_model: Writing instructions for how the computer should color and move things.
operators.example: Make a train look like it is melting, glowing, or shaking.

menu.MAT: Lets this material use settings from another material.

operators.explain_like_im_five: You tell the computer exactly how to draw, instead of using crayons it already knows.

operators.what_it_is: A material that brings another material into this one so it can be used or combined.
operators.mental_model: A plug that lets one material talk to another material.
operators.example: Use one material inside another to build a bigger setup.

menu.In: Chooses which material is being brought in.
menu.Label: A name so humans know what this connection is for.
menu.Connect Order: Decides which material connects first when there are many.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It lets one coloring tool use another coloring tool.

Line Drawing.Draw Lines: Turns line drawing on or off.
Line Drawing.Line Joint Type: Decides how corners connect when a line bends.
Line Drawing.Miter Threshold: Limits how sharp a corner is allowed to be.
Line Drawing.Line Start Cap Type: Shape used at the beginning of a line.
Line Drawing.Line End Cap Type: Shape used at the end of a line.
Line Drawing.Line Near Color: Color of the line when it is close to the camera.
Line Drawing.Line Near Alpha: How see-through the line is when close.
Line Drawing.Specify Line Far Color: Allows a different color when far away.
Line Drawing.Line Far Color: Color of the line when far away.
Line Drawing.Line Far Alpha: How see-through the line is when far away.

Point Drawing.Draw Points: Turns point drawing on or off.
Point Drawing.Far Alpha: Point see-through amount when far away.

Depth Setup.Depth Interpolation Model: Decides how size and color change with distance.

Width Control.Width Affected by FOV: Changes width based on camera view.
Width Control.Ortho Width: Width when camera has no perspective.

Arrow.Arrow Width: How wide the arrow head is.
Arrow.Arrow Height: How tall the arrow head is.
Arrow.Pullback: Moves arrow back from the end.

Attributes.Inner Dot Size: Size of dot inside a point.
Attributes.Attribute Type: What kind of stored information it is.

operators.what_it_is: A material that draws lines, points, and arrows instead of surfaces.
operators.mental_model: Drawing with markers instead of paint.
operators.example: Draw glowing train tracks or direction arrows.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It draws lines, dots, and arrows instead of coloring shapes.

operators.what_it_is: A material that does nothing and changes nothing.

menu.Null: Turns the pass-through on or off.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It lets things pass through without changing them.

operators.what_it_is: A material connector that lets materials link together inside a network.
operators.mental_model: A labeled plug that helps materials snap together in order.
operators.example: Organize several materials so they connect in the right sequence.

menu.N: Chooses which material connection this is.
menu.Label: A name so humans know what this connection is for.
menu.Connect Order: Decides which connection happens first if there are many.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It’s a labeled plug that helps materials connect in the right order.

operators.what_it_is: Marks which material is the final result that gets used.
operators.mental_model: The finish line where the material leaves the network.
operators.example: Choose which material is sent to the object.

menu.Out: Turns this output on or off.
menu.Select MAT: Chooses which material is sent out.
menu.Label: A name so humans know what this output is.
menu.Connect Order: Decides priority if there are multiple outputs.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It tells TouchDesigner which material to actually use.

PBR Alpha.Alpha Map: An image that decides which parts are see-through and which parts are solid.
PBR Alpha.Uniform Alpha: One single see-through amount for everything.
PBR Alpha.Alpha Front: How see-through the front-facing parts are.
PBR Alpha.Alpha Side: How see-through the side-facing parts are.
PBR Alpha.Alpha Rolloff: How smoothly see-through changes from front to side.

PBR Rim.Enable: Turns the rim light on or off.
PBR Rim.Color Map: An image that decides rim light color.
PBR Rim.Color Center: The main color of the rim light.
PBR Rim.Width: How thick the rim light edge is.
PBR Rim.Strength: How bright the rim light is.
PBR Rim.Edge Strength Map: An image that controls rim brightness at edges.

PBR Advanced.Darkness Emit Color: The glow color for dark areas.
PBR Advanced.Darkness Emit Map: An image that controls where dark glow happens.
PBR Advanced.Right Camera Space Depth To Alpha: Uses distance from the camera to control see-through.
PBR Advanced.Apply Point Color: Uses color stored on the object.
PBR Advanced.Instance Texture: Uses a different image per object copy.
PBR Advanced.Color Buffer: Reads color from a stored image buffer.
PBR Advanced.RGB: Uses red, green, and blue color data.

Phong RGB.Ambient Uses Diffuse: Uses the main color as the soft background color.
Phong RGB.Diffuse: The main paint color you see.
Phong RGB.Ambient: Soft light that fills everything evenly.
Phong RGB.Specular: Shiny highlights, like light on plastic.
Phong RGB.Emit: Makes the object glow by itself.
Phong RGB.Constant: Keeps the color steady no matter the light.
Phong RGB.Shininess: How tight and sharp the shiny spot is.

Phong Maps.Color Map: An image that decides color across the surface.
Phong Maps.Normal Map: An image that fakes bumps and dents.
Phong Maps.Bump Scale: How strong those fake bumps look.
Phong Maps.Enable Height Map: Turns on surface pushing using an image.
Phong Maps.Height Map: An image that pushes the surface up and down.
Phong Maps.Parallax Scale: How deep the surface illusion looks.
Phong Maps.Parallax Occlusion: Makes surface depth look more realistic.
Phong Maps.Displace Scale 1: How far the surface is pushed.
Phong Maps.Displace Midpoint: The middle level for pushing in or out.

Phong Environment.Environment Map: An image that pretends the object is inside a world.
Phong Environment.Environment Map Color: Color tint for the fake world reflection.
Phong Environment.Map 2D Type: How the world image is wrapped.
Phong Environment.Polygon Front Faces: Decides which side of the surface reacts.

operators.what_it_is: A classic material that uses simple lighting to make objects look shiny or dull.
operators.mental_model: Painting an object and shining a flashlight on it.
operators.example: Make a train look like plastic, metal, or rubber.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It colors something and makes it look shiny when light hits it.

operators.what_it_is: A material that draws tiny pictures on points instead of drawing surfaces.
operators.example: Turn points into glowing sparks or snowflakes.

menu.Color Alpha: Controls the color and how see-through each point is.
menu.Color Map: An image used as the sticker drawn on each point.
menu.Offset Left: Moves the sticker left on the point.
menu.Offset Right: Moves the sticker right on the point.
menu.Offset Bottom: Moves the sticker down on the point.
menu.Offset Top: Moves the sticker up on the point.
menu.Point Scale Attribute: Uses size information stored on the point.
menu.Constant: Uses one fixed size for all points.
menu.Attenuate Point Scale: Changes point size based on distance.
menu.Near Point Scale: Point size when close to the camera.
menu.Far Point Scale: Point size when far from the camera.
menu.Size Affected by FOV or Width: Changes size based on camera view or screen width.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It puts little pictures on dots and lets them grow or shrink.

operators.what_it_is: A material that chooses which parts are visible and which parts are hidden.
operators.mental_model: A stencil that decides what shows through.
operators.example: Only show certain areas of a material.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It decides what parts you can see and what parts you can’t.

operators.what_it_is: A material that switches between different materials.
operators.mental_model: A light switch that picks which tool is active.
operators.example: Change materials based on a number or condition.

menu.Extend: Decides what happens when the index goes out of range.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It picks one material from a list.

operators.what_it_is: A material that draws only the lines of a shape instead of filling it.
operators.example: Show the structure of a train without solid surfaces.

menu.Wireframe: Turns wire drawing on or off.
menu.Wireframe Mode: Decides how the lines are drawn.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It draws shapes using only lines.
